name: Otter AI
one_liner: AI meeting assistant that records, transcribes, summarizes, and surfaces action items from voice conversations and meetings.
access_notes: Otter.ai has long stated in its Help Center that it does not offer an open/public REST API. The only official programmatic surface is the Otter MCP Server (hosted at mcp.otter.ai), which uses OAuth with granular per-meeting permissions and connects AI assistants (Claude, ChatGPT) to a user's transcripts. It is positioned toward Enterprise but the OAuth connect flow itself is self-serve. NOTE: developer-guides.tryotter.com / helpdesk.tryotter.com belong to a DIFFERENT company (Otter restaurant-operations software), not Otter.ai.
api_notes: No documented public REST/GraphQL API for the transcription product. Official access is via the MCP server, which exposes a handful of read-oriented capabilities: search meeting transcripts, retrieve summaries, surface action items, and analyze conversation history. An unofficial community Python library (gmchad/otterai-api) reverse-engineers the private web API using email/password, but it is not supported.
mcp_notes: Otter ships an official, vendor-hosted remote MCP server at https://mcp.otter.ai/mcp, announced on the otter.ai blog and documented in its Help Center. OAuth-authenticated with granular permissions; listed in the Claude connector directory and works with ChatGPT and other remote-MCP clients. Community alternatives (e.g. DarrenZal/otter-mcp) also exist but are unofficial.
main_blocker: No public REST API to wrap; the only official programmatic path is Otter's own hosted OAuth MCP server (read-only transcript/summary/action-item access), so a Composio-style tool would ride on that MCP rather than a documented HTTP API.

evidence.claim: Otter ships an official MCP server (mcp.otter.ai) that connects meeting data to AI tools like Claude and ChatGPT, OAuth-authenticated with granular permissions.
Otter's Help Center hosts an 'Otter MCP Server' setup article covering connecting AI assistants to transcripts.
Otter Help Center article 'Does Otter offer an open API?' addresses the (historically absent) public API.
Unofficial reverse-engineered Otter.ai Python API exists using email/password auth against the private endpoints.

open_questions: Whether the official MCP requires an Enterprise plan or is available to free/paid individual tiers; whether Otter has quietly released any documented public REST endpoints beyond MCP; exact tool/scope list exposed by the MCP server.
